title: Baseline Deviation Detection
app: `Cisco Cyber Vision Splunk Add-On` (Splunkbase 5748) · `Nozomi Networks Universal Add-on` (Splunkbase 6905)
spl:
index=nozomi sourcetype="nozomi:alert" type_id="ANOMALY*"
| eval severity_label=case(risk>=8, "Critical", risk>=6, "High", risk>=4, "Medium", risk<4, "Low")
| stats count as total_deviations by severity_label, name, zone, _time
| sort -severity_label
| table _time, severity_label, name, zone, total_deviations
description: OT security platforms baseline normal network behavior — which devices talk to which, over what protocols, at what frequency. Deviations from baseline trigger alerts, detecting unauthorized communication changes, new data flows, or behavioral shifts that could indicate compromise or misconfiguration.
value: OT security platforms baseline normal network behavior — which devices talk to which, over what protocols, at what frequency. Deviations from baseline trigger alerts, detecting unauthorized communication changes, new data flows, or behavioral shifts that could indicate compromise or misconfiguration.
implementation: **Cyber Vision:** Create baselines for critical production zones; enable monitoring mode. **Nozomi:** Guardian automatically builds AI-powered behavioral baselines; anomaly alerts fire when deviations occur. Alert on any Critical or High severity deviations. Investigate and either acknowledge (legitimate change) or escalate (potential threat). Track unresolved deviations.
visualization: Deviation event timeline; baseline status dashboard; unresolved deviation count; severity distribution.
knownFalsePositives: Baseline deviations during plant turnarounds, recipe changes, or commissioning of new process areas.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Cisco Cyber Vision Splunk Add-On` (Splunkbase 5748) · `Nozomi Networks Universal Add-on` (Splunkbase 6905).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=cisco:cybervision:syslog` · Nozomi: `sourcetype=nozomi:alert` (type_id="ANOMALY").
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
**Cyber Vision:** Create baselines for critical production zones; enable monitoring mode. **Nozomi:** Guardian automatically builds AI-powered behavioral baselines; anomaly alerts fire when deviations occur. Alert on any Critical or High severity deviations. Investigate and either acknowledge (legitimate change) or escalate (potential threat). Track unresolved deviations.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=nozomi sourcetype="nozomi:alert" type_id="ANOMALY*"
| eval severity_label=case(risk>=8, "Critical", risk>=6, "High", risk>=4, "Medium", risk<4, "Low")
| stats count as total_deviations by severity_label, name, zone, _time
| sort -severity_label
| table _time, severity_label, name, zone, total_deviations
```

Understanding this SPL

**Baseline Deviation Detection** — OT security platforms baseline normal network behavior — which devices talk to which, over what protocols, at what frequency. Deviations from baseline trigger alerts, detecting unauthorized communication changes, new data flows, or behavioral shifts that could indicate compromise or misconfiguration.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=cisco:cybervision:syslog` · Nozomi: `sourcetype=nozomi:alert` (type_id="ANOMALY"). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Cisco Cyber Vision Splunk Add-On` (Splunkbase 5748) · `Nozomi Networks Universal Add-on` (Splunkbase 6905). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: nozomi; **sourcetype**: nozomi:alert. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=nozomi, sourcetype="nozomi:alert". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **severity_label** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by severity_label, name, zone, _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Pipeline stage (see **Baseline Deviation Detection**): table _time, severity_label, name, zone, total_deviations

Step 3 — Validate
Run the search for a time range that should include both routine and change activity, then compare the same assets, flows, and alerts in the Cisco Cyber Vision UI and in Nozomi Guardian or CMC, matching whichever source the event came from. Reconcile severities, counts, and key fields in both tools.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Deviation event timeline; baseline status dashboard; unresolved deviation count; severity distribution.
grandmaExplanation: We look for traffic and behavior that drifts from learned baselines so a quiet insider-style change is harder to hide.
